name: network
explanation: The Network check has no required configuration fields and no remote endpoint. It is host-local: the `Network` class dispatches to a platform-specific subclass (`LinuxNetwork`, `WindowsNetwork`, `BSDNetwork`, `SolarisNetwork`) and reads `/proc/net/dev` (and other `/proc` entries on Linux), uses psutil for socket/connection state, and optionally shells out to `ss` / `netstat` / `conntrack` / `ethtool` for richer stats. There is no host/port/URL/credential to discover. Auto-config is trivial: emit the integration with default empty instance whenever the Agent is running on a supported OS. Method `other` because there is no network probe — this is the same shape as the btrfs / iis pattern (host-local discovery).
notes: Optional features (conntrack, ethtool, AWS ENA, connection state via ss/netstat) require additional binaries or capabilities, but the base check works with no configuration on every supported OS. Conceptually identical to btrfs (pattern: host-local presence; no per-instance input).
